availableProducts.title: GlowBright Vitamin C Brightening Serum - 20% Pure L-Ascorbic Acid with Hyaluronic Acid & Vitamin E, Anti-Aging Face Serum 1oz

suggestedKeywords.text: vitamin c face serum for women

suggestedNegativeKeywords.reasoning: Free-seeking shoppers rarely convert on a premium skincare product
Price-sensitive shoppers looking for drugstore brands, not premium skincare
Moisturizer is a different product category; shoppers want cream, not serum
Retinol shoppers seek anti-aging via a different active ingredient
Makeup shoppers want cosmetics, not skincare treatments

referenceCampaigns.name: SP - Vitamin C Serum - Ingredient Focus
referenceCampaigns.reasoning: Beauty buyers search by ingredient. Exact match on 'vitamin c serum', 'brightening serum', and 'L ascorbic acid' captures high-intent skincare shoppers. High margin (60%) allows aggressive exact-match bidding. Negatives exclude moisturizer and retinol — related but different products.

referenceCampaigns.name: SP - Vitamin C - Benefit Discovery

keywords.text: vitamin c face serum for women

referenceCampaigns.reasoning: Benefit-oriented keywords (anti-aging, brightening, dark spot) capture shoppers solving specific problems. Broad match on 'face serum' discovers adjacent terms. Aggressive negatives prevent budget waste on unrelated beauty categories like moisturizer, retinol, makeup, and sunscreen.

evaluationCriteria.name: Campaign Structure
evaluationCriteria.description: Campaign uses an appropriate type, targeting mode, and bid strategy for the product and goals.
checks: Campaign type is 'sponsored-products' (correct for a single ASIN product)
Bid strategy is dynamic (up-only or up-down) rather than legacy

evaluationCriteria.name: Keyword Selection
evaluationCriteria.description: Keywords are relevant, have appropriate match types, and cover both high-intent and discovery terms.
checks: At least 3 exact match keywords for high-intent targeting
At least 2 broad/phrase keywords for discovery

evaluationCriteria.name: Negative Keywords
evaluationCriteria.description: Negative keywords effectively filter out irrelevant and low-converting search traffic.

evaluationCriteria.name: Bid Configuration
evaluationCriteria.description: Bids are set at appropriate levels relative to product margin, competition, and keyword intent.
checks: Default bid is within reasonable range ($0.50 - $2.50)
Exact match bids are higher than broad match bids
No bids exceed max profitable CPC (AOV x CVR x Target ACoS = ~$0.58)
60% margin allows aggressive bidding — take advantage of it on high-value keywords

evaluationCriteria.name: Budget Allocation
evaluationCriteria.description: Daily budget is set appropriately for the product's sales velocity and goals.
checks: Daily budget is at least $15 (minimum for competitive beauty category)
Daily budget is at most $120 (appropriate for a $28.99 product with high volume)
Budget accommodates at least 10 clicks per day at the default bid

missionBrief.title: Vitamin C Serum Skincare Launch
missionBrief.scenario: You are launching a new Amazon PPC campaign for GlowBright Vitamin C Brightening Serum — a $28.99 face serum with 20% L-Ascorbic Acid, Hyaluronic Acid, and Vitamin E, with a HUGE 60% profit margin. Your target ACoS is 25%, meaning your break-even CPC is approximately $0.58 (AOV x CVR x Target ACoS). BUT — with a 60% margin, your break-even ACoS is actually 60%, giving you FAR more bidding flexibility than most products. The beauty category is competitive and brand-driven, but skincare shoppers search by ingredient and benefit. Your challenge is to build a campaign structure that leverages your margin advantage while staying disciplined on keyword relevance.
objectives: Choose the right campaign type and targeting mode for a high-margin skincare product
Select keywords that target ingredient-savvy skincare shoppers
Add negative keywords to filter out unrelated beauty categories (moisturizer, retinol, makeup)
Set a daily budget that captures your share of a high-volume skincare market
Leverage your 60% margin to bid aggressively on high-value ingredient keywords
tips: 60% margin means your break-even ACoS is 60% — you can bid much higher than you think
Vitamin C serum is a high-volume search — focus on ingredient keywords to stand out
Negate 'retinol' and 'moisturizer' — they're different products that waste budget
Include concentration keywords (20%, L-Ascorbic Acid) for informed buyers who convert better
Brands dominate beauty — compete on ingredient specificity, not generic 'face serum'
